title: Reading - Part 1
instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.heading: Poetry Afternoon
questions.text: Students can read their own poems or a favourite poem by another writer. Please give your name to the librarian by Wednesday. The event will only happen if at least ten students take part.
questions.question: What does the poster say?

options.B: Enough students must join for the event to happen.
options.A: Students may only read poems they wrote themselves.
options.C: The librarian will choose every poem on Wednesday.

questions.explanation: The event needs at least ten students to take part.

questions.heading: Message from Toby to Jen
questions.text: Jen, I’ve finished reading the book, but I left it at home. I can tell you the ending at lunch if you need it for your homework, or I’ll bring the book tomorrow.
questions.question: What is Toby offering?

options.C: To help Jen before he returns the book.
options.A: To lend Jen the book during lunch today.
options.B: To finish reading the book for homework.

questions.explanation: Toby can tell Jen the ending before bringing the book tomorrow.

questions.heading: Science Club Trip
questions.text: The visit to the planetarium is now full. Students who have paid should collect information sheets from Room 2. Anyone still interested can add their name to the waiting list.
questions.question: What should interested students understand?

options.A: They may get a place only if one becomes free.
options.B: They can still pay and join the trip immediately.
options.C: They should collect information sheets after paying.

questions.explanation: The trip is full, but students can join a waiting list.

questions.heading: From: Lucy / Subject: Your photos
questions.text: The photos of the school garden are beautiful, but they are too dark for the website. Could you take a few more tomorrow morning when the light is better?
questions.question: What does Lucy want?

options.B: New photos taken in better conditions.
options.A: The garden website to be made darker.
options.C: Photos of a different school garden.

questions.explanation: Lucy asks for more photos when the light is better.

questions.heading: Music Shop Lessons
questions.text: Our guitar lessons are usually individual, but students can share a lesson with a friend to reduce the cost. Shared lessons must be booked for six weeks at a time.
questions.question: What does the advert say?

options.C: Learning with someone else can be cheaper.
options.A: All guitar lessons are for groups only.
options.B: Shared lessons can be booked weekly.

questions.explanation: Sharing a lesson with a friend reduces the cost.
